revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=思子淵神社| 所在地=滋賀県高島市安曇川町中野564| 祭神=思子淵神
彦火火出見尊| 社格=村社| 創建=建武元年| 例祭=[[5月1日]]| 神事=}} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する神社である。旧社格は村社。 ==ご祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==ご神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武]]元年の創祀と伝わる。[[シコブチ|思子淵神]]は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　5月1日 ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: ページ [[思子淵神社（高島市)]] を [[思子淵神社]] へ移動: [[WP:NC]]
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=思子淵神社| 所在地=滋賀県高島市安曇川町中野564| 祭神=思子淵神
彦火火出見尊| 社格=村社| 創建=建武元年| 例祭=[[5月1日]]| 神事=}} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する神社である。旧社格は村社。 ==ご祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==ご神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武]]元年の創祀と伝わる。[[シコブチ|思子淵神]]は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　5月1日 ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=思子淵神社| 所在地=滋賀県高島市安曇川町中野564| 祭神=思子淵神

彦火火出見尊| 社格=村社| 創建=建武元年| 例祭=[[5月1日]]| 神事=}} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する神社である。旧社格は村社。 ==祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年の創祀と伝わる。[[シコブチ|思子淵神]]は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　5月1日 ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 ==アクセス== * JR[[安曇川駅]]より[[江若バス]]朽木学校行　中野バス停下車徒歩8分 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 思子淵神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]思子淵神社本殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[高島市]][[安曇川町]]中野564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|08.9|N|135|58|36.0|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 思子淵神
彦火火出見尊 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 建武元年（1334年） |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 七シコブチ |例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = }} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）の創祀と伝わる。思子淵神は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　5月1日 ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 ==交通== * JR[[安曇川駅]]より[[江若バス]]朽木学校行　中野バス停下車徒歩8分 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 思子淵神]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 「[[思子淵神社]]」を「[[思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町)]]」へ移動: 提案後特に意見も付かなかったため、原案に従って（但し、精確を期して高島市追加）

彦火火出見尊 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 建武元年（1334年） |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 七シコブチ |例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = }} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）の創祀と伝わる。思子淵神は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭　　5月1日 ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 ==交通== * JR[[安曇川駅]]より[[江若バス]]朽木学校行　中野バス停下車徒歩8分 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 思子淵神]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

[[ja:%E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shortest 100 pages as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]][[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]] [[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]][[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]] [[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]][[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]] {{神社 |名称 = Shikobuchi Shrine (思子淵神社) |画像 = [[ファイル:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]Main hall of Shikobuchi Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}}, Azugawa-chō, Nakano 564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|8.65|N|135|58|36.51|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}}
{{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |例祭 = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社}} under the former shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} * {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of {{ill|Kenmu (Japan)|ja|建武}} (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with {{ill|Kappa (folklore)|ja|河童}} legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9年}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (例祭) – {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Azumigawa Station|ja|安曇川駅}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[ja:思子淵神社]]

It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社}} under the former shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} * {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of {{ill|Kenmu (Japan)|ja|建武}} (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with {{ill|Kappa (folklore)|ja|河童}} legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9年}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Azumigawa Station|ja|安曇川駅}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with {{ill|Kappa (folklore)|ja|河童}} legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9年}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Azumigawa Station|ja|安曇川駅}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社}} under the former shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} * {{ill|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of {{ill|Kenmu (Japan)|ja|建武}} (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with {{ill|Kappa (folklore)|ja|河童}} legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9th year }} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Azumigawa Station|ja|安曇川駅}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称 = 思子淵神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]思子淵神社本殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|8.65|N|135|58|36.51|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 思子淵神
彦火火出見尊 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 建武元年（1334年） |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 七シコブチ |例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = }} '''思子淵神社'''（しこぶちじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]安曇川町中野に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[シコブチ|思子淵神]] * [[彦火火出見尊]] ==神紋== 菱形四ッ目 ==歴史== [[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）の創祀と伝わる。思子淵神は、[[安曇川]]流域固有の神様である。「[[七シコブチ]]」と呼ばれる思子淵神を祀る神社のひとつ。七シコブチには、[[河童]]伝説を有する。[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に村社に列格された。 ==祭事== * 例祭 [[5月1日]] ==境内社== * 八幡神社 * 稲荷神社 ==交通== * JR[[安曇川駅]]より[[江若バス]]朽木学校行　中野バス停下車徒歩8分 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 思子淵神]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しこふちしんしや たかしまし}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |name = Shikobuchi Shrine (思子淵神社) |image = [[File:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]Main hall of Shikobuchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], Azugawa-chō, Nakano 564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|8.65|N|135|58|36.51|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 9|qq=}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:%E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In {{ill|Meiji 9|ja|明治9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1876年}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:%E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:%E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{神社 |name = Shikobuchi Shrine (思子淵神社) |image = [[File:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]Main hall of Shikobuchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], Azugawa-chō, Nakano 564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|8.65|N|135|58|36.51|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:%E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:E6%80%9D%E5%AD%90%E6%B7%B5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%AB%98%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%89%E6%9B%87%E5%B7%9D%E7%94%BA%E4%B8%AD%E9%87%8E)]] [[sn:hikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[sn:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shikobuchi Jinja (Takashima City, Azumi-gawa Town, Nakano)]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11491513]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Shikobuchi Shrine (思子淵神社) |image = [[File:Shikobuchi-n.JPG|center|250px]]Main hall of Shikobuchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], Azugawa-chō, Nakano 564 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|8.65|N|135|58|36.51|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11491514}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11491514}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Kenmu 1 (1334) |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri) |別名 = |札所等 = Nana Shikobuchi (七シコブチ) |festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] |神事 = }} '''Shikobuchi Shrine''' (思子淵神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Nakano district of Azugawa-chō. It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. It is one of the seven shrines known as "Nana Shikobuchi" (七シコブチ), all of which enshrine this deity. The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa (folklore)]] legends. In [[:en:1876|Meiji 9]] (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – [[:en:May 1|May 1]] == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Access == * From JR [[:en:Adogawa Station|Azumigawa Station]], take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11491514}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the former shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} == Shrine Crest == The shrine's crest is a four-eyed rhombus (菱形四ッ目). == History == Shikobuchi Shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu (Japan)]] (1334). {{ill|Shikobuchi|ja|思子淵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a deity unique to the Azumigawa River region. In {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=Meiji 9}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Adogawa Station|en|Adogawa Station|WD=Q4684027|lt=Azumigawa Station}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11491514}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The Nana Shikobuchi shrines are associated with {{ill|Kappa (folklore)|en|Kappa (folklore)|WD=Q335140|lt=Kappa (folklore)}} legends. In {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=Meiji 9}} (1876), the shrine was officially designated as a village shrine (村社). == Festivals == * Annual festival (festival) – {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}} == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} == Access == * From JR {{ill|Adogawa Station|en|Adogawa Station|WD=Q4684027|lt=Azumigawa Station}}, take the {{ill|Kōjaku Bus|ja|江若バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjaku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江若交通|1=Kojak Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551295}} bound for Kuchiki Gakkō, get off at Nakano Bus Stop, and walk 8 minutes. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1380 Shikobuchi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|version=94423703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:思子淵神社]] [[ja:思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11491514}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
